title: Authentication service outage affecting all users
summary: Complete authentication service outage lasting 33 minutes. All users were unable to log in or access authenticated endpoints. Root cause was an expired TLS certificate on the internal service mesh.
customer_impact_summary: 100% of users unable to log in or access any authenticated functionality for 33 minutes during peak morning hours (US East).
internal_impact_summary: All internal tools requiring authentication were also unavailable. Support team unable to access customer data to respond to inquiries.
detection_details: PagerDuty alert triggered on auth-service error rate exceeding 50% threshold. Alert fired 3 minutes after first errors appeared.
root_cause: Internal mTLS certificate for auth-service expired. Certificate was provisioned manually 2 years ago during initial service mesh setup and was not included in automated certificate rotation.
contributing_factors: Manual certificate provisioning during initial setup bypassed automated rotation
No alerting on certificate expiration for internal services
Certificate inventory did not include internal service mesh certificates
Runbook for certificate rotation assumed all certs were in automated system
prevention_gaps: Certificate monitoring only covered external-facing certificates
Service mesh setup documentation did not mention manual certificate step
No periodic audit of certificate inventory completeness
resolution_summary: Rotated expired certificate manually, then added all internal certificates to automated rotation system. Verified all services recovered and sessions were restored.
what_went_well: Detection was fast (3 minutes from first error to alert)
On-call engineer identified root cause within 10 minutes
Communication to stakeholders was clear and timely
Rollback was not needed - forward fix was straightforward
what_went_wrong: Initial investigation looked at wrong service (api-gateway) for 5 minutes
No documented process for emergency certificate rotation
Status page update was delayed by 15 minutes
lessons_learned: All certificates must be in automated rotation - no exceptions
Certificate monitoring must cover internal and external certs
Quarterly certificate inventory audits should be mandatory

timeline.description: First TLS handshake failures appear in auth-service logs
PagerDuty alert fires: auth-service error rate > 50%
On-call engineer acknowledges alert and begins investigation
Initial focus on api-gateway - no issues found
Identified TLS certificate expiration in auth-service mesh config
Incident commander engaged, war room opened
All services healthy, error rates back to baseline

hypotheses.description: API gateway configuration change caused auth failures
TLS certificate expiration in service mesh

action_items.description: Add all internal certificates to automated rotation system
Implement certificate expiration alerting for all certificates
Update service mesh documentation with certificate requirements

evidence.description: TLS handshake failure logs showing certificate expiration
Error rate spike graph during incident
Original service mesh setup ticket showing manual cert creation
